the city Jail. No notice has come,

Mayor Pratt. If you are good citi-

mittee investigate our affidavits

Woman's club is given praise, not

To Acting Chief Sullivan, Mrs.
Stafford addresses the following:


Acting Chief of Police Sullivan
In the Inland Herald of February
"a you are quoted as saying: Mrs.
Stafford, who made the charges, is
an ardent I. w. w. sympathizer,
and sat with Gurley Flynn through
most of her two trials. Many of the
other women who "stormed" the
city hall were also in sympathy
with the I. w. W. Chief Sullivan,
you heard me state under oath at
the first trial of Elizabeth Gurley
Flynn that l was not an I. w. w.
Did you wilfully forget? Not one
woman who attended the council
meteing on the night of February %
was an I. w. W., and to most of
them no worse epithet could be
hurled than I. w. w. sympathizer.
How does it happen, Chief Sullivan,
that you can say so authoritatively
with whom these women sympa-
thize? Were you even there?


81 was not on the Mrs. Jones
bond. The records will prove that.
I attended both trials and I was SUBJ
poenaed as witness. Are you so
ignorant of legal procedure that
you do not know that sympathiZers
cannot sit with a defendant unless
they happen to be his or her coun-
sci?


There is one point we have in
common. that is that an investiga-
ton of the city Jail is needed. We,
the club, however, do not want
whitewash investigation, but a real
one Examining witnesses. reading
affidavits of those unfortunate
enough to have been within the city
Jail.


"Chief Sullivan, l have made no
charges against you, or any of your
officers. l did not know whether
these conditions existed with or
without your knowledge. You were
put in office TO KNOW! 1t makes
no difference to the citizens of Spo-
kane, and it makes no difference to
unfortunate residents of the city
Jail whether you were ignorant Of
conditions there, or whether you
were cognizant of the entire truth.
If you did not know. you were in
competent; If you did know you
were guilty. Which was 1t. Acting
Chief Sullivan? You can't dodge
the issue much longer. can you?
Mrs. Philip p. Stafford"
